title: DefaultTransport name implies configurability it lacks
description: DefaultTransport is a unit struct whose only behaviour is to call the private helper default_reqwest_client(). The name 'DefaultTransport' implies it is one option among many configurable transports, but because it is a ZST with a single fixed implementation it is really just a named entry-point for that helper. Two alternatives: (a) delete DefaultTransport entirely and expose a free function JmapChatClient::new_with_default_transport (or a Default impl on TransportConfig via a blanket) so callers don't need to name an otherwise-opaque type; (b) if the ZST must stay because it is the most common public API entry, rename it PlainTransport or StandardTransport to signal 'publicly-trusted TLS, no customisation' rather than implying it is the fallback/configurable baseline. Suggested rewrite of the factory site: instead of JmapChatClient::new(DefaultTransport, NoneAuth, url) expose JmapChatClient::new_plain(auth, url) that internally calls default_reqwest_client() without surfacing the ZST in caller code.
close_reason: Added new_plain(auth, url) convenience constructor to JmapChatClient; DefaultTransport hidden from common-case call sites
